audit_metadata.note: Baseline version. Re-run the audit after edits and save as v2 for diff.

findings.issue: Success criteria tests assert on committed JSON artifacts, not recomputed behavior
findings.description: test_rf_f1/test_xgb_auc/test_lr_auc/test_modularity/test_gb_r2_threshold open DATA_RESULTS/rq*_success_metrics.json and assert on values already written there (e.g. test_rq2_success.py:5-8 json.load then assert metrics['rf_f1'] >= 0.70). They never invoke run_rq2/run_rq1/run_rq3, so they validate the contents of a file the author generates, not actual model performance.
findings.impact: Headline success claims are self-certified by editable output files. A stale or hand-edited JSON makes every threshold test pass green without any model achieving the metric.
findings.remediation: Convert to behavioral tests: run the model on a fixed fixture inside the test (or a session-scoped fixture calling run_rqX once) and assert on freshly returned metrics. At minimum assert the metrics JSON is newer than source/data.

findings.issue: test_ensemble_beats_solo is vacuous and misnamed (dead assertion)
findings.description: Lines 24-34 compute best_solo = max(xgb_auc, rf_f1, lr_auc) but never use it. The only assertions are that the predictions parquet exists, has rows, and contains 'ensemble_risk_prob'. The claimed comparison (ensemble > solo) is never tested.
findings.impact: A core comparative claim is asserted by name but unverified; the test list misleads reviewers into thinking it is covered.
findings.remediation: Compute ensemble AUC on a held-out set and assert it exceeds best_solo, or rename the test and remove the dead best_solo variable.

findings.issue: Leakage guards pass vacuously when data is absent
findings.description: test_leakage.py:5-6 'if feature_df.is_empty(): return' and :11 'if train_df.is_empty() or test_df.is_empty(): return'; test_no_target_leakage.py:27-29 'if not path.exists(): return'. Without the gitignored parquet data these tests early-return and report PASS.
findings.impact: Leakage controls are the integrity backbone of the dissertation, yet in CI or a fresh checkout they become no-ops that always pass, giving false assurance.
findings.remediation: Use pytest.skip() with a reason instead of silent return so missing data shows as SKIPPED not PASSED, and add data-independent unit tests with synthetic fixtures.

findings.issue: Louvain fallback hardcodes modularity = 0.35, trivially passing the success threshold
findings.description: When NetworkX/python-louvain fail to import (caught silently at lines 16-24), algo2_louvain() returns a hardcoded modularity of 0.35 (lines 119, 128, 130) and assigns 'communities' by buyer_region instead of running community detection. run_rq1 writes metrics['modularity']=0.35, exceeding RQ1_MODULARITY_THRESHOLD (0.30) and passing test_modularity. The import is fragile on this runtime (Python 3.14.1 needs a stdlib monkeypatch).
findings.impact: On a runtime where NetworkX does not load, reported modularity is a constant literal, not a measured value, a fabricated thesis metric indistinguishable from a real one.
findings.remediation: Do not emit a fixed modularity in the fallback; compute it from the region-partition or set modularity=None and fail loudly / surface networkx_available=False. Pin a NetworkX version that imports on the target interpreter.

findings.issue: Production imports missing from requirements (matplotlib, python-pptx)
findings.description: src/eda/visualizations/generate_charts.py imports matplotlib and phase2_ppt.py imports pptx (python-pptx), but neither is in requirements.txt. pyproject.toml declares no [project.dependencies], so requirements.txt is the only manifest.
findings.impact: A clean pip install -r requirements.txt then chart/PPT generation raises ModuleNotFoundError; EDA visualization and presentation-export stages do not reproduce on a fresh environment.
findings.remediation: Add matplotlib>=3.8 and python-pptx>=0.6 to requirements.txt (and mirror runtime deps into pyproject [project.dependencies]). Re-verify in a fresh venv.

findings.issue: Runtime exec() of reconstructed CPython stdlib source, silently swallowed
findings.description: Lines 8-17 read dataclasses._add_slots source via inspect.getsource, string-replace a line, and exec(_source, dataclasses.__dict__) to monkeypatch the stdlib at import time for the whole process, wrapped in 'except Exception: pass'. Works around a Python 3.14.1 bug while requires-python is '>=3.11'.
findings.impact: Fragile and opaque: the patch is keyed to an exact stdlib source string, so a future 3.14.x silently no-ops (bare except) and the crash resurfaces with no diagnostic. Executing reconstructed code that mutates stdlib globals is a code-injection-shaped pattern and a reproducibility hazard.
findings.remediation: Remove the monkeypatch; pin a supported interpreter (requires-python '>=3.11,<3.14') or upgrade the dep triggering the slots bug. If unavoidable, scope it narrowly, log on failure, and gate on exact sys.version_info.

findings.issue: Binary imbalance logic applied to a 3-class target
findings.description: y = risk_label.astype(int) with values {0,1,2} (feature_engineering.py:104-115) is multiclass, but scale_pos_weight uses n_pos=(y_train==1) and n_neg=(y_train==0) (lines 47-50), ignoring class 2, and ensemble_predict/_positive_class take only proba[:,-1].
findings.impact: XGBoost imbalance correction uses binary semantics on a multiclass target, mis-weighting class 2 and conflating 'class 2 probability' with 'risk'.
findings.remediation: Either binarize risk_label (>=1 -> at-risk) before training, or handle multiclass properly (per-class sample_weight, multi:softprob, defined risk aggregation).

findings.issue: _proba fallback indexes one-hot by raw label value
findings.description: Lines 174-179: fallback builds np.eye(len(classes))[pred] using predicted label values as row indices. Non-contiguous labels (e.g. {0,2}) index out of range or map to the wrong column.
findings.impact: Potential IndexError or silently incorrect probability matrix for any estimator without predict_proba on a sparse label set.
findings.remediation: Map labels through fitted class index (np.searchsorted(model.classes_, pred)) rather than positional indexing.

findings.issue: Division by row count without empty guard
findings.description: Lines 84-85 compute 100*n_high/len(unified) and 100*n_anom/len(anomalies). Empty unified_risk_scores.parquet or rq3_anomaly_flags.parquet raises ZeroDivisionError on page load.
findings.impact: Dashboard crashes on the landing page when a results table is empty rather than degrading gracefully.
findings.remediation: Guard denominators (pct = 100*n_high/len(unified) if len(unified) else 0.0) and short-circuit the KPI row when frames are empty.

findings.issue: Published risk scores are in-sample for training contracts
findings.description: Reported metrics use X[test_mask] correctly, but ensemble_predict runs over the full X including training rows (line 126) and writes them to rq2_governance_predictions.parquet as deployment risk scores.
findings.impact: Per-contract risk scores in the dashboard/integration are optimistically biased for the majority of contracts the models trained on; in-sample scores presented as out-of-sample.
findings.remediation: Use out-of-fold predictions (cross_val_predict) or emit scores only for the held-out period; document training-period scores as in-sample.

findings.issue: Anomaly residuals computed in-sample over the training split
findings.description: The GB regressor is fit on a 75% train split (line 27) but residuals/anomaly flags use all_pred over the entire dataset (lines 42-45), so training rows get artificially small residuals.
findings.impact: gb_flag and the consensus anomaly set are biased toward flagging the 25% test rows, distorting anomaly rates and lof_gb_overlap used for RQ3 success.
findings.remediation: Generate residuals via cross_val_predict (out-of-fold) or compute anomalies only on the held-out portion.

findings.issue: SQL assembled via f-string interpolation of file paths
findings.description: _register_views interpolates self.parquet_path into read_parquet('{...}') (lines 43, 57) and copy_query into COPY (...) TO '{output_path}' (line 97). Same pattern in tests/test_data_quality.py:5,12,22. parquet_path comes from PGI_PARQUET_PATH env var (config.py:29).
findings.impact: Low (operator/env-controlled, not external user input), but a path containing a single quote breaks the string literal and injects SQL into the DuckDB connection.
findings.remediation: Use DuckDB parameter binding (read_parquet(?)) or validate/quote-escape the path before interpolation.

findings.issue: Data-derived identifier rendered into HTML with unsafe_allow_html
findings.description: Line 89 interpolates supplier_id into st.markdown(f'...{supplier_id}...', unsafe_allow_html=True). supplier_id is selectbox-bounded but the underlying values come from external OpenTED data and render unsanitized as HTML.
findings.impact: Stored-XSS shape: markup in a source identifier renders in the browser. Bounded (internal tool, selectbox) but the unsanitized-data-into-HTML pattern is unsafe.
findings.remediation: Render dynamic values via st.write/st.subheader (auto-escaped) or html.escape() them; reserve unsafe_allow_html for static markup.

findings.issue: Tests assert only file existence, not content
findings.description: test_chart_artifacts_exist/test_report_artifacts_exist only check .exists() for HTML/PNG/JSON/MD; test_feature_artifacts_exist only checks parquet files exist. No schema/row-count/value assertions.
findings.impact: Empty, truncated, or corrupt artifacts pass; coverage is limited to 'a file is present'.
findings.remediation: Add content assertions (non-zero size, expected columns/keys, plausible row counts) to a representative artifact per type.

findings.issue: test_shap_top_features couples to a feature ranking and references SHAP that is never computed
findings.description: Asserts 'single_bid_rate' is in rf_top3_features+xgb_top3_features (lines 41-47), but no SHAP is computed anywhere (metric is sklearn feature_importances_, rq2_governance.py:194-202). Hard-codes an expected top feature.
findings.impact: Misleadingly named; brittle to legitimate model changes; and since single_bid_rate proxies the bid_count<=1 label condition, asserting it as a top predictor risks rewarding residual leakage.
findings.remediation: Rename to reflect feature_importances_, drop/justify the hard-coded feature, and add a correlation/leakage check between single_bid_rate and the label-defining bid_count condition.

findings.issue: Test temporal split differs from the production split
findings.description: train_df/test_df fixtures use award_year <= 2016 and >= 2019 (conftest.py:45-48) and test_train_test_temporal_split asserts the 2016/2019 boundary, but the model's configured split is TRAIN_YEARS=2014-2018 and TEST_YEARS=2019-2020 (config.py:40-42).
findings.impact: The temporal-leakage test validates a boundary the model never uses, so it cannot catch leakage across the real 2018/2019 train/test boundary.
findings.remediation: Derive fixture boundaries from config.TRAIN_YEARS/TEST_YEARS so the test tracks the production split.

findings.issue: Near-trivial thresholds and sample-tuned bounds
findings.description: test_centrality_distribution re-normalizes inside the test and asserts top_centrality > 0.003 (passes for almost any non-degenerate distribution); test_dependency_rate asserts a hand-tuned range 0.20<=at_risk<=0.45 specific to the current sample.
findings.impact: The centrality test is effectively a no-op; the dependency-rate test is brittle and will fail on a valid but different sample without meaningfully validating RQ1.
findings.remediation: Assert against the documented threshold (RQ1_CENTRALITY_TOP10_THRESHOLD) on production-normalized values; replace the magic range with a justified data-relative bound.

findings.issue: Unvalidated int() of environment variables at import time
findings.description: Lines 30-31, 35-37: ROW_LIMIT/GRAPH_EDGE_LIMIT/STRATA_* are int(os.environ.get(...)) at module import. A non-numeric value (e.g. PGI_ROW_LIMIT='all') raises ValueError during import of config.py.
findings.impact: A malformed env var crashes every entry point that imports config (the whole pipeline and tests) with an import-time traceback rather than a clear configuration error.
findings.remediation: Validate conversions with a descriptive error, or a helper that falls back to default on parse failure with a logged warning.

findings.issue: Declared-but-unused package: shap
findings.description: shap>=0.44.0 is declared but 'import shap'/'shap.' appears nowhere in src or phase scripts. test_shap_top_features (its only conceptual reference) uses sklearn feature_importances_, not SHAP.
findings.impact: Install/build weight and a misleading methodology signal (implies SHAP explainability that is not implemented).
findings.remediation: Remove shap from requirements, or implement the SHAP analysis the test name and docs imply.

findings.issue: Heavy notebook deps declared but unused by the pipeline; runtime deps unpinned
findings.description: jupyter, jupyterlab, nbconvert are declared but not imported in src/ or phase scripts (a notebooks/ dir exists, so possibly dev-only). All runtime deps use floating >= bounds except plotly==5.18.0 and kaleido==0.2.1, and pyproject.toml declares no dependencies.
findings.impact: Reproducibility drift: a fresh install can resolve newer, behavior-changing versions of polars/sklearn/duckdb; unused heavy deps inflate the environment.
findings.remediation: Move notebook tooling to an optional/dev extra, pin runtime deps to tested versions (or commit a lockfile), and mirror runtime deps into pyproject [project.dependencies].

findings.issue: Prompt-assumed paths not present in repository
findings.description: The audit scope referenced a configs/ directory; none exists (config lives in src/core/config.py, .env.example, pyproject.toml, requirements.txt, Makefile). data/raw/IT_DIB_2023.{csv,parquet} are symlinks to ../../Dataset/ outside the repo, and data/ is gitignored.
findings.impact: No defect, recorded for completeness. The external-symlink data source means reproduction depends on a path outside version control.
findings.remediation: Document dataset acquisition/placement in README (a download or symlink-setup script) so data lineage is explicit and reproducible.
